title: Butterflied Lamb Leg with Rice & Tzatziki
subtitle: Seasoned butterflied lamb leg slow-roasted for control, then finished hard in cast iron for browned edges. Built for rice, tzatziki, and lemon.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: This assumes a pre-seasoned lamb leg. The key is drying the surface, cooking gently, then finishing hot.
items: 1 seasoned butterflied lamb leg, about 2.5 lb
1–2 tbsp neutral oil or olive oil, for searing
1 tbsp butter, optional
1 lemon, for finishing
Cooked rice, for serving
Tzatziki, for serving
Chopped parsley, dill, or cilantro, optional
ingredients.callout: Doneness target: pull from the oven around 125–130°F, then sear to finish around 135–140°F.

shoppingList.item: 1 seasoned butterflied lamb leg, about 2.5 lb

body.html: A juicy, seasoned butterflied lamb leg with browned skillet edges, served over rice with cool tzatziki and lemon.

items: Pat the lamb dry so it browns instead of steams
Roast gently at 325°F until mostly cooked
Rest briefly so juices settle
Sear hard in cast iron for crust
Slice thin against the grain and finish with lemon

body.html: Serving vibe: rice underneath, sliced lamb on top, tzatziki on the side, lemon over everything.

slides.title: Dry & Temper the Lamb
slides.checkboxLabel: Remove from package, pat dry, rest 30–45 min

items: Remove lamb from the package and discard excess marinade liquid.
Pat the surface lightly dry with paper towels, but do not scrape off the seasoning.
Let sit at room temperature 30–45 minutes while the oven heats.

body.html: Drying matters: wet marinade will steam in the skillet and block browning.

items: Preheat oven to 325°F.
Place lamb on a rack over a sheet pan, or directly in a shallow roasting pan.
Roast until the thickest part reaches 125–130°F.

body.html: Expect roughly 25–45 minutes depending on thickness. Use temperature, not the clock.
Move the lamb to a plate or board and rest 5–10 minutes before the skillet finish.
This short rest helps prevent a flood of juices when the meat hits the hot pan.

items: Heat cast iron over medium-high to high until very hot.
Add a thin film of oil.
Sear lamb 1–2 minutes per side without moving it.
If using butter, add it near the end and baste for 30 seconds.

body.html: You are not trying to cook it much more here — just build browned edges and a little char.

items: Check the thickest part after searing.
Aim for 135–140°F for a juicy medium finish.
Rest 5 minutes before slicing.

body.html: If it is already at temp after the oven, sear faster and harder — crust only.

slides.title: Slice & Serve
slides.checkboxLabel: Slice against grain, serve with rice + tzatziki

items: Slice lamb thinly against the grain.
Spoon over rice.
Add tzatziki on the side or drizzle lightly.
Finish with fresh lemon and herbs if using.

body.html: Balance rule: if the lamb tastes heavy, add lemon. If it tastes flat, add a tiny pinch of salt.
